assistant.user: So I had a meeting with my new professor in charge of the Summer internship. In my previous Winter internship, I was developing a hexapod, and the project failed due to multiple reasons, mainly due to poor gear design and insufficient motor torque. Initially, I had planned to develop a quadruped having 3 servos per leg, but my then project in charge, Binsu Sir, advised me to develop a 6-legged robot. However, we remained with the 12 motor design and kept on researching the quadruped design. While reviewing a few research papers, I stumbled upon a technical drawing, which immediately sparked a room of thought of developing a hexapod by reducing a motor for each leg, and we developed a Hexapod with just 12 motors, otherwise needing 18 motors in all. However, the motors we selected could not bear such high amounts of load, and the mechanism I designed was poorly designed. Therefore, the project failed. Now the faculty in charge of my project had been a part of certain mishaps in the college faculty politics, and has been disturbed since. This all happened in December 2025; it is now April 2026. Now he is on the verge of being fired, and obviously, his students are being shifted under a new person in charge, so I have been shifted under Shubham Sir's guidance. Now we had a meeting with Shubham Sir today. In the meeting, he told us what's going on with Binsu Sir and why we were actually shifted under him. In the meeting, we pitched our current internship project plan, which involves developing a custom cycloidal drive gearbox attached to a 1000kV BLDC motor to address the torque issue. Along with that, we plan to develop a custom PCB, program an MCU and build a board equivalent to ESP32 because ESP32 is Chinese-made. After reviewing the idea, the sir said how can we justify the project? What good will it bring to the college table? He also stated that since Binsu Sir will no longer be in charge, his subordinates will be affected along with him, so the unrest caused by his leaving will be let out on us if we do not have a concrete justification supporting our project. He asked us what our Hexapod will do after the development. He understood the purpose of the development of the Hexapod, about how we can help the disaster response teams, miners and soldiers, but his main question was what the Hexapod would do after immediate development? Currently, we have no justification as to why we are developing the hexapod. He stated that he can get us in contact with multiple faculty members at the college handling robotics, indirectly indicating that internal faculty politics are in unstable conditions, and it is better for us to be under the guidance of multiple faculty members rather than being under just one. He also asked us if there are competitions being held where we can participate and gain some publicity towards the college, and so that will benefit us both, our resumes and the college's image. So I am really clueless as to what we can do?
